name: Gut inflammation memory sensor (tetrathionate + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for tetrathionate, a biomarker of gut inflammation. The validated TtrSR sensor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917. Complements the thiosulfate sensor as a second inflammation marker.

input.operatingRange: TtrSR responds over physiological inflamed-gut tetrathionate levels (validated in mouse colitis)

sensing.sensorMechanism: The TtrS sensor kinase activates the response regulator TtrR in response to tetrathionate, turning on its target promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Tetrathionate -> TtrS/TtrR activates PttrR -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT), or pair with a memory switch (cI/Cro) for a persistent record of an inflammation episode.

parts.name: TtrS/TtrR two-component system
parts.source: ported to E. coli Nissle by Daeffler et al. 2017 / Riglar et al. 2017
parts.notes: Tetrathionate sensor; validated in vivo in mouse gut.

parts.name: sfGFP / mCherry reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

output.result: Fluorescent-cell fraction reflects gut tetrathionate / inflammation.

performance.limitOfDetection: TtrSR module validated in vivo (mouse gut inflammation).
performance.notes: The tetrathionate sensor (with a genetic memory switch) is validated in vivo by Riglar et al.; the CRISPRi integration here is a design template. Pairs with the thiosulfate sensor for two-marker inflammation sensing.

safety.biocontainment: Built in probiotic E. coli Nissle; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA (and optional memory switch)
buildSteps.description: Target the reporter promoter; optionally add a cI/Cro memory element to record transient inflammation.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: TtrS/TtrR. TU2: PttrR -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9 + reporter. Use a stable low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate in vitro
buildSteps.description: Confirm tetrathionate response across a standard curve before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the validated TtrSR tetrathionate sensor in E. coli Nissle with a dCas9 CRISPRi circuit
partsSources: Riglar et al. 2017, Nat. Biotechnol. (tetrathionate memory sensor in Nissle)
Daeffler et al. 2017, Mol. Syst. Biol. (sulfur-compound sensors)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
